message:
fix(test): correct active_summary assertion in cross-module integration test

active_summary() returns only non-terminal tokens. After advancing t1 to
Completed and t2 to Failed, only t3 (InProgress) remains active. Assert
active_count()==1 and total_count()==3 instead of summary.len()==3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2mvmx","title":"Watcher clients + per-client view-state model for agent swarms","description":"## Goal\nAdd explicit watcher-client semantics (read-only observers) and per-client view state (active tab/pane/mirror mode) to support multi-agent sessions safely.\n\n## Why\nZellij’s watcher + per-client state model maps directly to FrankenTerm multi-agent orchestration and reduces contention/ambiguity around shared focus.\n\n## Scope\n- Define client roles: interactive vs watcher (read-only).\n- Track per-client view state (active tab/pane, mirrored or independent view).\n- Enforce role-based policy for mutating actions (, pane kill, workflow mutation).\n- Surface role/view information in  and session APIs.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Session/client model update + policy hooks + API exposure.\n- Tests for role enforcement and multi-client focus semantics.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- Watchers cannot perform mutating actions.\n- Interactive clients preserve current behavior.\n- State visibility is deterministic for orchestration tools.\n\n## Cross-references\n- Zellij session analysis: \n- Session persistence epic: \n- /dp integration epic: ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:42:57.379276Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:24.185984Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:24.166081Z","close_reason":"Superseded by wa-3jewu (same intent, corrected prefix/description)."}
 {"id":"ft-3rwhd","title":"Cross-subsystem action completion tokens + cause-chain context","description":"## Goal\nImplement a logical completion protocol for multi-step operations spanning capture/storage/policy/workflow/event bus, with end-to-end cause-chain context.\n\n## Why\nPrevents racey partial completion and improves debuggability under load; mirrors Zellij route-thread completion-token pattern.\n\n## Scope\n- Add completion token object carried across subsystem instructions.\n- Define logical completion boundaries for key operations (, workflow-triggered actions, recovery actions).\n- Propagate cause-chain context through internal channels and audit records.\n- Add timeout + failure semantics with explicit terminal states.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Runtime contract doc + implementation for completion tokens.\n- Tests covering success, timeout, and partial-failure paths.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- No operation reports success before logical completion boundary.\n- Timeout/failure paths preserve enough context for postmortem.\n- No deadlock/regression in existing workflow execution paths.\n\n## Cross-references\n- Zellij inventory analysis: \n- Fork hardening epic: \n- asupersync epic: ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:42:57.369688Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:24.171386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:24.171372Z","close_reason":"Superseded by wa-33uf8 (same intent, corrected prefix/description)."}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb","title":"[EPIC] Universal mux I/O flight recorder + hybrid lexical/semantic search","description":"# [EPIC] Universal Mux I/O Flight Recorder + Hybrid Search (FrankenSQLite + Tantivy + Semantic)

## Vision
Build a **flight recorder** for FrankenTerm that captures **all text entering and leaving the mux server** in an ultra-performant append-only pipeline, then exposes fast, trustworthy, hybrid lexical/semantic retrieval for operators and agents.

This epic is intentionally broader than simple logging. It defines a full system:
1. Capture/tap points for ingress and egress text
2. Durable append-only write path under high concurrency
3. Indexing and retrieval architecture (lexical + semantic)
4. Query/API surfaces for humans and robots
5. Reliability, observability, and rollout safety

## Why this matters
- Multi-agent terminal swarms generate high-volume, high-value forensic data.
- Current observability is good for event/state workflows, but not yet a true end-to-end immutable transcript plane optimized for forensic replay and hybrid retrieval.
- A robust flight recorder increases debugging speed, incident response quality, postmortem accuracy, and model/operator feedback loops.

## Design intent
- **Performance first**: append-only, batched writes, minimal per-event overhead.
- **Correctness first**: total ordering/causality metadata sufficient for replay and audit.
- **Durability first**: resilient write path (including exploration of FrankenSQLite multi-writer and WAL self-healing capabilities).
- **Search first**: Tantivy-backed lexical retrieval plus semantic retrieval and rank fusion.
- **Operator first**: stable CLI/robot/MCP interfaces and runbooks.

## Research grounding
Implementation must be informed by close study/adaptation patterns from:
- `/dp/frankensqlite`
- `/dp/coding_agent_session_search` (cass)
- `/dp/xf`

## Non-goals (for this epic)
- Replacing all existing storage paths in one unsafe big-bang migration.
- Shipping semantic retrieval without lexical fallback and measurable quality baselines.
- Adding unbounded retention without explicit cost/retention controls.

## Deliverables
- Full bead graph (research -> architecture -> implementation -> validation -> rollout)
- Feature-gated implementation plan with rollback path
- Search quality/perf test strategy and success thresholds
- Operator docs and migration guidance

## Success criteria
- Flight recorder captures ingress+egress text with stable ordering metadata.
- Append-only writer sustains swarm-scale load without destabilizing watcher/runtime.
- Lexical and hybrid search return correct results with measurable latency/quality targets.
- End-to-end system is testable, observable, and safe to roll out incrementally.

## Acceptance criteria
- All child tracks are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Dependency graph is acyclic and implementation-ready for swarm execution.
- Every major component includes unit/integration/e2e/load tests and failure-mode coverage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:26.151022Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:26.151022Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","search","semantic","tantivy"]}
-{"id":"ft-oegrb.1","title":"Research track: deep source-study + adaptation plan for frankensqlite/cass/xf","description":"## Track objective
Create a rigorous, implementation-grade understanding of `/dp/frankensqlite`, `/dp/coding_agent_session_search` (cass), and `/dp/xf`, then translate that into directly actionable adaptation guidance for FrankenTerm.

## Why this track exists
Premature implementation without deep source-level study risks cargo-culting and architecture drift. This track produces the factual foundation for all subsequent tracks.

## Deliverables
- Deep-dive dossiers per source project
- Cross-project architecture extraction matrix
- Adapt-vs-rewrite decisions with risk/cost rationale
- Final ADR describing the target flight-recorder/search architecture

## Acceptance criteria
- No downstream implementation bead should require re-reading external repos to understand scope.
- All key assumptions and tradeoffs are documented with explicit reasoning and test implications.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.090904Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.090904Z","labels":["cass","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","research","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.090904Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.1","title":"Study /dp/frankensqlite deeply for append-only recorder suitability","description":"## Goal
Perform a code-level architectural study of `/dp/frankensqlite` focused on applicability to flight-recorder ingestion at swarm scale.

## Background
We need evidence-based decisions about adopting FrankenSQLite capabilities (multi-writer concurrency, WAL durability model, self-healing behaviors, operational characteristics) instead of speculative integration.

## Scope
- Map write path internals relevant to append-only transcript ingest.
- Identify integration seams (API boundaries, transaction model, backpressure behavior, checkpointing).
- Characterize durability and recovery semantics, including WAL fault scenarios.
- Document required adapter layer in FrankenTerm.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained technical dossier with architecture diagrams, hot-path notes, risks, and recommended integration patterns.
- "Adopt as-is vs adapt vs avoid" table for key FrankenSQLite subsystems.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dossier includes concrete code references and explicit assumptions.
- At least one proposed integration path and one fallback path are described.
- Open risks are converted into explicit downstream beads.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.499014Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:53:26.478185Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:53:26.47817Z","close_reason":"Dossier delivered: frankensqlite architecture extraction and adaptation guidance complete","labels":["flight-recorder","frankensqlite","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.499014Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed deep source study of /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankensqlite and published dossier at docs/flight-recorder/frankensqlite-append-log-dossier.md. Key extraction delivered: append-only segment + torn-tail recovery patterns, bounded backpressure primitives (bulkhead/two-phase queue), WAL-FEC async repair pipeline pattern, and copy/adapt/avoid guidance for ft-oegrb tracks 3/4/7/8.","created_at":"2026-02-12T07:53:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.2","title":"Study /dp/coding_agent_session_search (cass) two-tier search architecture","description":"## Goal
Perform a deep source study of `/dp/coding_agent_session_search` (cass), emphasizing its two-tier architecture (raw store + search layer) and hybrid lexical/semantic patterns.

## Background
cass is directly relevant to our target capability. We need precise extraction of what to copy/adapt and what to re-implement for FrankenTerm constraints.

## Scope
- Map ingest pipeline and data normalization strategy.
- Map Tantivy schema/analyzers, query semantics, and ranking behavior.
- Map semantic retrieval components and fusion strategy.
- Identify assumptions that do not hold for live mux flight-recorder data.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained cass architecture dossier.
- Candidate reusable modules/patterns list with compatibility score.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dossier includes direct mapping from cass concepts to FrankenTerm components.
- Explicit list of "copy/adapt/avoid" decisions with rationale.","notes":"Completed deep source study of cass two-tier architecture. Added dossier at docs/flight-recorder/cass-two-tier-architecture-dossier.md with: ingest/storage/search mapping, lexical/semantic/hybrid flow, assumptions mismatch for live mux data, and explicit copy/adapt/avoid decisions + compatibility scores.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.532976Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:22:23.863782Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:22:23.863735Z","labels":["cass","research","search","semantic","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.532976Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.3","title":"Study /dp/xf deeply for scalable hybrid retrieval design patterns","description":"## Goal
Perform a deep source study of `/dp/xf` with focus on high-volume indexing/search, hybrid retrieval behavior, and production-operational search patterns.

## Background
xf provides a second reference implementation with potentially different tradeoffs than cass. We need both to triangulate a robust design.

## Scope
- Map data pipeline stages and indexing strategy.
- Map lexical and semantic retrieval plumbing.
- Map ranking logic, query APIs, and performance controls.
- Extract reliability and operational patterns relevant to FrankenTerm.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained xf architecture dossier.
- Delta analysis vs cass (what xf does better/worse for our use-case).

## Acceptance criteria
- Dossier clearly identifies portable patterns and non-portable assumptions.
- Cross-project comparison hooks are prepared for the synthesis bead.","notes":"Completed deep xf source-study and added dossier artifact at docs/flight-recorder/xf-hybrid-retrieval-dossier.md with architecture mapping, portability analysis, and detailed delta-vs-cass synthesis hooks for ft-oegrb.1.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.565787Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:29:57.327657Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:29:57.327586Z","labels":["hybrid","research","search","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.565787Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","title":"Build cross-project extraction matrix (frankensqlite vs cass vs xf)","description":"## Goal
Synthesize findings from frankensqlite/cass/xf into a single adaptation matrix that directly drives implementation sequencing.

## Background
Separate studies are not enough; we need a unified decision artifact that translates research into implementation-ready work packages.

## Scope
- Build a matrix by subsystem: ingest/write durability/indexing/query/ranking/ops.
- For each subsystem: identify source project precedent, adaptation cost, risk, and test implications.
- Produce explicit recommendations for FrankenTerm target architecture.

## Deliverables
- Architecture extraction matrix (self-contained and implementation-oriented).
- Traceability table linking each recommendation to downstream beads.

## Acceptance criteria
- Matrix includes risk/complexity scores and dependency hints.
- Downstream track owners can implement without revisiting source repos.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.598965Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:12.429143Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:12.429129Z","close_reason":"Cross-project extraction matrix delivered and mapped to downstream beads","labels":["architecture","planning","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.598965Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.6938Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.723532Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.752861Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed cross-project synthesis matrix and saved deliverable to docs/flight-recorder/cross-project-extraction-matrix.md. Matrix merges frankensqlite durability patterns, cass search architecture patterns, and xf operational patterns into implementation-default decisions and sequencing mapped to ft-oegrb tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","title":"Author ADR: target flight-recorder architecture + phased implementation map","description":"## Goal
Write an ADR for the target flight-recorder + hybrid-search architecture and lock first implementation increment boundaries.

## Background
A formal decision record reduces churn and prevents drift once implementation starts across multiple agents.

## Scope
- Define canonical architecture for v1 rollout.
- Define phased implementation sequence (MVP -> hardened -> scale).
- Define explicit non-goals, deferrals, and rollback boundaries.
- Define success metrics and gate criteria per phase.

## Deliverables
- ADR with decision rationale and alternatives considered.
- Sequenced execution map and handoff checklist for implementation agents.

## Acceptance criteria
- ADR is self-contained and references only stable bead artifacts.
- All implementation tracks have explicit entry conditions and dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.631865Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:55:00.093104Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:55:00.09309Z","close_reason":"ADR and phased implementation map delivered","labels":["adr","architecture","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.631865Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.782153Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Published ADR + phased implementation map at docs/flight-recorder/adr-0001-flight-recorder-architecture.md. Decision set includes four-plane architecture, canonical append-log source-of-truth, lexical-first + hybrid expansion strategy, governance gates, and phase-based rollout criteria mapped to ft-oegrb tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:56Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","title":"Run feasibility spikes for writer/indexer/hybrid rank hot paths","description":"## Goal
Execute focused technical spikes that validate the highest-risk assumptions before full implementation begins.

## Background
Research and ADR reduce uncertainty, but spikes are needed to validate hot-path feasibility and integration complexity.

## Scope
- Spike append-only write throughput envelope with representative event volume.
- Spike Tantivy incremental indexing from append log snapshots.
- Spike hybrid query fusion behavior on a small truth-set.

## Deliverables
- Spike report with measured metrics and pass/fail against target budgets.
- Updated risk register and recommended sequencing adjustments.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one spike per risk category (write path, indexing, ranking).
- Failures convert into explicit blocker beads before implementation proceeds.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.81142Z","labels":["performance","search","spike"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.811381Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}

Conclusions and implementation threshold recommendations are documented for downstream tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:01:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.2","title":"Capture track: canonical ingress/egress event schema + mux tap semantics","description":"## Track objective
Define and implement the canonical flight-recorder event model and capture tap points for **all mux ingress/egress text** with deterministic ordering metadata.

## Why this track exists
If capture semantics are ambiguous, durability and search become untrustworthy. This track establishes the contract for what is captured, in what order, and with what context.

## Deliverables
- Event schema (ingress/egress/control markers/metadata)
- Tap-point implementation plan for mux boundaries
- Causality/sequence model across panes and workflows
- Backpressure/failure semantics for capture stage

## Acceptance criteria
- Event schema is versioned, testable, and replay-compatible.
- Capture contract is explicit enough for independent indexing/search teams to implement against it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645175Z","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","title":"Define versioned recorder event schema for ingress/egress mux text","description":"## Goal
Define a versioned canonical event schema for mux ingress/egress text capture, including metadata needed for replay, search filtering, and causality analysis.

## Background
Schema ambiguity will invalidate downstream indexing and replay. This bead establishes the ground-truth contract.

## Scope
- Define event types (ingress text, egress output, control markers, lifecycle markers).
- Define required metadata (pane/session/workflow correlation IDs, timestamps, sequence numbers, source).
- Define schema evolution/versioning rules and compatibility tests.

## Deliverables
- Recorder event schema spec and serialization contract.
- JSON-schema or equivalent testable schema artifact.

## Acceptance criteria
- Schema is precise enough for independent producer/consumer implementation.
- Evolution rules cover additive and breaking changes explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:46.35793-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:46.35793-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented recorder event schema types in recording.rs: RecorderEvent, RecorderEventPayload (4 variants), RecorderEventSource, RecorderEventCausality, 6 supporting enums, RECORDER_EVENT_SCHEMA_VERSION_V1 constant, parse_recorder_event_json() with version validation. All 5 schema contract tests pass. JSON schema at docs/flight-recorder/ft-recorder-event-v1.json verified.","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","title":"Implement ingress tap points for all mux-bound text/action injections","description":"## Goal
Implement ingress capture tap points (text/actions entering mux/panes) with minimal overhead and explicit source attribution.

## Background
Ingress visibility is required for full forensic replay and for understanding operator/agent actions leading to outcomes.

## Scope
- Identify ingress boundaries and hook locations.
- Capture payload + metadata in canonical schema.
- Ensure capture path does not break policy gating behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ingress capture implementation plan + instrumentation tests.
- Validation that policy decisions and ingress logs remain consistent.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ingress events are captured for all supported injection paths.
- Overhead budget for ingress capture is documented and met in benchmarks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:45:26.001155-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:45:26.001155-05:00","close_reason":"Ingress tap integrated into PolicyGatedInjector with 8 integration tests","labels":["capture","ingress","mux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864528Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
@@ -1080,6 +1080,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-orzc","title":"E2E: profile switch + rollback","description":"## Scenarios
- Create profile from current config
- Apply profile with dry-run preview
- Rollback to previous profile

## Logging
- Capture diff output and config snapshots

## Success Criteria
- E2E artifacts show consistent diffs and safe rollback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanForge","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:07:09.630531569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.276612-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-05T15:38:27.443635987Z","close_reason":"E2E script added and verified: config profile create/list/diff/apply/rollback (scripts/e2e_config_profiles.sh).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-orzc","depends_on_id":"wa-v3k5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-orzc","depends_on_id":"wa-f0i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-orzc","depends_on_id":"wa-ts1a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-ou4o","title":"Implement MCP resources (wa://panes, events, accounts, workflows, rules)","description":"# Task: Implement MCP resources

## Goal
Expose read-only resources that mirror what a human would see in `wa status/events/accounts/...` but in a machine-friendly way.

## Resources
- `wa://panes` — current registry + inferred state
- `wa://events` — recent events (optionally unhandled)
- `wa://accounts` — known accounts + usage snapshot
- `wa://workflows` — available workflows + descriptions
- `wa://rules` — enabled packs + pattern list
- **`wa://reservations` — active pane reservations (owner/TTL/reason)**

## Requirements
- Resources must be **read-only**.
- Resources should be cheap to compute; prefer cached snapshots from the watcher.
- JSON output must be stable and versioned.

## Deliverables
- Resource handlers registered in MCP server.
- Pagination options where needed (events).

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.1.5`:
  - schema stability (versioned)
  - parity checks vs robot/human query results where applicable
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can browse resources without invoking tools.
- Output remains stable across versions (schema changes require version bump).


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ bd-4vx.3.5: Persist events, agent_sessions, workflow_executions, workflow_step_log ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.4.1: Pane registry + discovery loop (wezterm list, fingerprinting, lifecycle) ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.1.1: Workflow engine types: Workflow trait, StepResult, WaitCondition, WorkflowContext ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.5: [EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth) ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.2.1.4: Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ bd-nu4.3.1.5: MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs ● P2
","notes":"Completed MCP resource implementation with base+templated resources and validated behavior:

 {"id":"wa-oud5","title":"MCP audit integration: record each tool call decision/outcome (redacted)","description":"# Task: MCP audit integration

## Goal
Ensure MCP usage is trustworthy by recording an audit record for each MCP tool call.

MCP is high-risk because it is typically invoked by an agent. We need:
- a trace of what was requested
- why it was allowed/denied
- what was actually executed

## Requirements
- For each MCP tool invocation:
  - record tool name
  - record a redacted summary of arguments
  - record policy decision and preconditions
  - record outcome (success/error) and elapsed time
- Link audit entries to any underlying action audit entries (send/workflow).

## Redaction
- Do not store secrets or full inputs.
- Store hashes/summaries consistent with the shared redaction system.

## Testing
- MCP tests (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`) must cover:
  - tool call emits an audit record on allow AND deny
  - redaction behavior on representative arg payloads
  - linkage between MCP audit record and underlying action audit record (when applicable)
  - reservation tool calls (`wa.reserve`/`wa.release`) emit audit rows

## Acceptance Criteria
- Calling an MCP tool always results in an audit entry, even when denied.
- Audit entries are sufficient to debug a failed MCP automation without reproducing.


LABELS: area-audit, area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ bd-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.7: Audit trail emission: record allow/deny for every action (send, workflow steps, MCP) with redaction ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1.3: Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs ● P1

 {"id":"wa-p23t","title":"Tests: pane_uuid stability across rename/move/session churn","description":"# Task: Tests for pane_uuid stability

## Goal
Ensure stable identity remains stable across realistic churn.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - marker/user-var parsing is robust
  - fingerprint fallback collision behavior is understood and bounded
- Integration tests:
  - registry maintains stable identity across:
    - title changes
    - cwd changes
    - tab moves
    - pane disappearance/reappearance

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover the churn cases that historically cause "wrong pane" actions.

 {"id":"wa-p3i","title":"E2E script: watch-and-notify mode (--notify-only events → notifications)","description":"# E2E script: watch-and-notify mode (`wa watch --notify-only`)

## Goal
Validate end-to-end that notify-only mode:
- detects events and emits notifications
- **does not auto-handle** (no workflows run, events remain unhandled)
- respects notify filters and throttling
- includes suggested actions in notifications

This is the integration proof for notify-only mode (`wa-spi`) + notifications (`wa-psm`) under the standard E2E harness.

## Key constraints
- Deterministic: **no fixed `sleep N`**. Use bounded waits (polling with timeouts) and a quiescence detector.
- Safe shutdown: watcher processes must be stopped gracefully and reliably (SIGINT/`wa stop`), with bounded waits.
- No global config mutation: use an isolated E2E workspace/config.
- Artifacts must be redacted (no transcript leakage).

## Test setup
- Start a harness-owned mock webhook server with:
  - `/received` to read captured notifications as JSON
  - `/attempt_count` (optional)
  - request logging to artifacts
- Start WezTerm mux + dummy panes that can deterministically emit known events.
- Start `wa watch` in notify-only mode in a dedicated E2E workspace:
  - `wa watch --notify-only …`
  - notifications configured to point to the mock server

## Scenarios

### 1) Notify-only mode does not auto-handle
- Start watcher: `wa watch --notify-only --workspace <e2e> …`
- Trigger a known event that would normally be auto-handled (e.g., compaction):
  - via dummy pane output (preferred) OR a harness `wa e2e emit` helper
- Wait until mock server has ≥1 received payload (poll with timeout).
- Assertions:
  - notification payload exists and references the correct `rule_id` / pane
  - `wa events --unhandled --format json` still lists the event (still unhandled)
  - `wa workflow executions` (or DB evidence) shows **no** workflow ran for that event

### 2) Notification filter
- Start watcher with a filter:
  - `wa watch --notify-only --notify-filter "usage_limit" …`
- Trigger two events:
  - one matching filter (usage limit)
  - one non-matching (compaction)
- Wait for quiescence (bounded).
- Assert mock server received exactly one notification, for the matching event.

### 3) Throttling in notify-only mode
- Configure a low throttle budget in the E2E config.
- Trigger a burst of N events quickly (deterministic dummy output).
- Wait for quiescence (bounded).
- Assert:
  - delivered notifications ≤ configured budget
  - watcher logs contain explicit “throttled” markers for skipped notifications

### 4) Suggested action included
- Trigger compaction.
- Wait for first notification.
- Assert the notification body includes a suggested remediation (e.g., `wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane <id>`).

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) with prerequisites and default inclusion status.

## Artifacts
- `wa_watch_notify_only.log`
- `notifications_received.json` (redacted)
- `events_unhandled.json`
- `workflow_executions_slice.json` (or other “no workflow ran” evidence)
- `mock_server.log`

## Logging contract
Every step logs:
- case/scenario name
- watcher PID/workspace
- mock server bind address
- timeouts used

Example:
```
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] watcher started workspace=e2e pid=12345 notify_only=true filter=usage_limit
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] emit rule_id=codex.usage_limit_reached pane=3
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] wait received>=1 timeout=10s
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] assert event still unhandled: OK
```

## Testing
- Determinism:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - all waits are “wait until condition” with explicit timeouts
- Correctness:
  - notification exists and matches expected `rule_id` + `pane_id`
  - event remains unhandled (no workflow execution)
  - filter and throttling behave as configured
- Shutdown reliability:
  - stop watcher via `wa stop --workspace <e2e>` when available, otherwise SIGINT + bounded wait
  - artifacts always include watcher exit status and last log lines

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Notify-only mode delivers notifications for detected events.
- [ ] Notify-only mode does not auto-handle (events remain unhandled, no workflows executed).
- [ ] Filters and throttling work deterministically.
- [ ] Notifications include suggested actions.
- [ ] Artifacts/logs are sufficient to debug failures.","notes":"Completed: added watch_notify_only scenario to E2E registry + harness (notify-only baseline, filter, throttling, suggested action checks) and updated e2e integration checklist. E2E script not executed here (requires WezTerm/mux).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderSnow","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:15.541602048Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:43:53.93705561Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:43:53.93688875Z"}
 {"id":"wa-p48pw","title":"Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_client.rs (997 LOC) is the most complex async module. Manages persistent Unix socket connections with custom binary protocol.

## Current tokio usage
- tokio::net::UnixStream for socket connection
- tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} for framed I/O
- tokio::time::timeout for 5s connect/read/write timeouts
- tokio::sync::mpsc for pane output subscription
- tokio::sync::watch for cancellation signaling
- tokio::spawn + tokio::select! for background polling

## Migration order
1. Replace UnixStream with asupersync net
2. Replace AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt with asupersync I/O traits
3. Replace timeouts with Budget::deadline(Duration::from_secs(5))
4. Replace mpsc/watch with asupersync channels (two-phase send)
5. Replace tokio::spawn with scope.spawn for pane output subscription
6. Replace select! with asupersync::combinator::select for poll+cancel

## Critical: Wire protocol MUST NOT change
PDU framing: 4-byte big-endian length prefix + PDU type byte + payload.
The framing logic must produce identical wire format. This is non-negotiable.

## subscribe_pane_output() migration (hardest part)
```rust
// Becomes a scoped task:
cx.region(|scope| async {
    let (tx, rx) = asupersync::channel::mpsc::channel(capacity);
    scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
        loop {
            cx.checkpoint()?;  // Cancellation point
            let delta = fetch_pane_delta(cx).await?;
            let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;
            permit.send(delta);
        }
    });
    rx
}).await
```

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime + real sockets)
1. **Connect**: connect to mock Unix socket, verify stream established
2. **PDU round-trip**: write PDU frame, read back, verify bit-for-bit identical
3. **PDU partial read**: simulate slow writer, verify buffered reading assembles complete frame
4. **Connect timeout**: unreachable socket, verify Budget::deadline triggers Cancelled
5. **Read timeout**: connected but silent peer, verify read deadline triggers Cancelled
6. **Write timeout**: connected but full buffer, verify write deadline triggers Cancelled
7. **Pane output subscription**: mock server sends deltas, verify channel receives all
8. **Subscription cancellation**: cancel subscription mid-stream, verify no orphan tasks, no leaked obligations
9. **Two-phase channel safety**: cancel between reserve and send, verify no message loss
10. **Concurrent operations**: multiple requests over same connection, verify serialization
11. **Error recovery**: simulate broken pipe, verify appropriate error propagation

Each test logs: connection events, PDU encode/decode, timeout triggers, channel reserve/commit, scope enter/exit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All DirectMuxClient ops work through asupersync
- PDU wire format bit-for-bit identical (regression test)
- Timeout behavior preserved (5s each)
- Pane output subscription uses structured concurrency
- 11+ tests covering all scenarios
- Obligation leak oracle passes for all tests

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent client operations testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent DirectMuxClient operations:
  - Multiple pane subscriptions active simultaneously — verify no cross-talk between subscription channels
  - Interleaved PDU reads and writes on the same connection — verify framing integrity
  - Concurrent connect attempts — verify connection serialization
  - Race between cancellation and PDU commit — verify no partial PDU writes

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for client operation latency**: Add `benches/mux_client_ops.rs` measuring:
  - PDU encode + write_all latency for typical payload sizes (64B, 1KB, 64KB)
  - PDU read_exact + decode latency for typical payload sizes
  - Full round-trip latency (send command PDU → receive response PDU) over Unix socket
  - Pane subscription setup latency (scope.spawn + channel creation)
  - Comparison with tokio-based DirectMuxClient for equivalent operations

## Cross-references
- See wa-brc7d.5 (mux crate in asupersync FrankenTerm crates epic) — DirectMuxClient is the core of the mux crate and its performance characteristics directly determine the crate's viability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:15.845522Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:49:29.703468Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-q8vj3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:58.776523Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:58.893985Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-1yz79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:59.011099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-p48pw","depends_on_id":"wa-1bznu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:59.3449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
